beats.narration: You learn what each casting flaw looks like on film. Then the Level Two job: accept or reject, by comparison.

payload.title: GRADING CASTINGS
payload.sub: The verdict is comparison, not artistry.

beats.narration: First, identification. A void passes more radiation and prints darker; dense inclusions print lighter. The shape is the fingerprint: porosity round, shrinkage irregular, cracks sharp.
Match the set: steel climbs E four forty six, E one eighty six, E two eighty; aluminum and magnesium take E one fifty five.

payload.heading: Match the set to metal and thickness

beats.narration: Plates come graded, typically one through five. Lay the production film beside the specified level; judge the worst area. Equal or better passes; worse fails.

payload.heading: Worst area versus the specified plate
payload.note: EQUAL OR BETTER → PASS · WORSE → FAIL

beats.narration: Three categories get no plates: cracks, hot tears, inserts. Under typical specifications, no level is acceptable. Found means rejected.

payload.sub: Cracks, hot tears, inserts — no acceptable level.
